jobs.description:
Overview

The Director of Development (DD) leads the strategy, execution, and performance of all fundraising efforts that support the overall mission and growth of the Boys \& Girls Clubs of Muncie (BGCM). The DD is also responsible for overseeing marketing and communications to ensure alignment with organizational goals and to strengthen visibility, engagement, and donor support.

Description

Mission: To inspire and enable all young people, especially those who need us most to reach their full potential as productive, caring, and responsible citizens.

Job Description

Job Title: Director of Development

FLSA Classification: Exempt

Salary Range: $48,000 \- $58,000

Reports to: CEO

Supervisory responsibilities: Development Staff

Summary/objective The Director of Development (DD) leads the strategy, execution, and performance of all fundraising efforts that support the overall mission and growth of the Boys \& Girls Clubs of Muncie (BGCM). The DD is also responsible for overseeing marketing and communications to ensure alignment with organizational goals and to strengthen visibility, engagement, and donor support.

Essential duties and responsibilities include the following and may be completed with or through subordinate personnel. Other duties may be assigned. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these essential functions.

Every staff member is expected to cultivate and maintain positive donor relationships.

· Create, lead, implement, and execute a comprehensive development strategy, including oversight of marketing and public relations efforts.

· Identify, create, and maintain opportunities to increase public awareness of Club services, programs, and impact.

· Lead all corporate and individual giving efforts, including expanding strategies such as major gifts, planned giving (estate planning, bequests), and recurring giving programs.

· Partner with the CEO and Board of Directors to develop and execute donor engagement strategies, including face\-to\-face visits, stewardship plans, and annual campaign initiatives. Maintain and grow relationships with donors, prospects, foundations, and other funding sources.

· Serve as an ambassador and spokesperson for the organization, clearly communicating the mission and impact of BGCM to internal and external audiences.

· Lead grant development efforts, including prospect research, proposal writing, grants calendar management, and reporting progress to the CEO.

· Provide leadership and vision for fundraising events and campaigns that drive revenue and community engagement.

· Participate in meetings as appropriate and communicate effectively in both written and verbal formats. May present to the Board of Directors, stakeholders, or community partners as needed.

· Engage in professional development opportunities and networking events as approved by the CEO.

Work environment

· Able to stand and/or walk up to 25% of time. Able to sit up to 50% of the time. Able to view a computer monitor for 6\-8 hours per day. Some travel may be required in company or personal vehicle. Must be able to lift up to 25 pounds at times. The role may be eligible for some hybridized/WFH opportunity at the discretion of the CEO, and based upon the availability of technology.

· Employee required to possess a valid driver’s license and is occasionally required to transport members in a company vehicle.

· Proof of vehicle insurance is required.

Education and/or Experience

· Bachelor’s degree in finance, business administration, communications, nonprofit management, or a related field; or equivalent relevant experience (minimum of 4 years) required.

· Experience in fundraising, development, or advancement is strongly preferred.

· Grant writing or grant management experience is preferred.

· Familiarity with and passion for serving youth, particularly at\-risk populations, is preferred.

· Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to engage diverse audiences.

· Proven ability to meet deadlines and manage multiple priorities effectively.

· Experience using data to inform strategy and decision\-making.

· Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail.

· Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) or similar systems required.

· Experience using donor management systems (e.g., Bloomerang or similar CRM platforms) to track donor activity, manage pipelines, and inform fundraising strategy is preferred.

· High level of professionalism, discretion, and confidentiality required.

Compensation \& Benefits

Competitive salary based on experience

Paid time off, including vacation, sick leave, and holidays

Retirement plan options with 3% company match

Professional development and networking opportunities

Health, vision, and dental

Pay: $48,000\.00 \- $58,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance

Application Question(s):

* Please list at least 3 professional references.

Education:

* Bachelor's (Preferred)

Experience:

* development: 4 years (Preferred)

Ability to Commute:

* Muncie, IN 47302 (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Deputy Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
**About Democratic National Committee** **We’re hiring! Are you fired up to elect Democrats up and down the ticket?****Position Summary:*** The DNC is seeking a highly motivated Deputy Chief Operating Officer (Deputy COO) to provide support to and partner with the Chief Operating Officer (COO) in the execution and management of all hands\-on operational aspects and day\-to\-day functions of the Committee. Critical for success at this point in the campaign cycle, this position will assist the COO in providing both leadership and operational HR expertise across the organization, as well as help manage expansion of processes throughout the midterm cycle and beyond for our work with coordinated campaign efforts.The Deputy COO will act as a thought partner and support in developing strategic initiatives that meet the needs of a fast paced organization and election cycle.

**Responsibilities:*** Assist the COO in providing overall operational leadership of the Committee in formulating and achieving objectives in areas such as human resources, staff and building security, travel, events, risk management, contracting, diversity equity and inclusion (“DEI”), state support for state coordinated campaigns, asset\-tracking;
* Collaborate with the COO on matters involving compliance and insurance activities;
* Provide counsel to the COO on significant matters affecting internal and external operations and policies, including major organizational events;
* Support the COO in spearheading the development, recommendation, implementation, and communication of new or revised systems, policies, and procedures;
* Collaborate with the COO in developing and implementing short\-range and long\-range goals and business plans, along with measures of effectiveness, ensuring proper internal controls and procedures;
* Represent the COO and the DNC, as designated, in meetings before DNC, community and business group meetings;
* In cooperation with the COO, build and maintain relationships with external partners and stakeholders, as appropriate;
* Provide direct support to and monitor activities of assigned departments to ensure conformance with goals and objectives of reporting unit and eliminate impediments to peak performance;
* Assist in the management of Operations Facility Management teams;
* Assist in the maintenance, support and promotion of a safe work environment while complying with all of the DNC’s safety rules, policies, and procedures; and
* Other duties and special projects as assigned to support the department and the DNC's mission.

 **Qualifications:*** Have a passion for and commitment to the DNC mission
* Have a minimum of a Bachelors degree
* Present strong operational experience: ideally has worked in operational management for a socially responsible organization for 4\+ years and have some experience working in political organizations
* Display experience in or ability to handle matters of organizational development, personnel management, budget and resource development, and strategic planning.
* Enjoy a fast paced working environment, adapt quickly to shifting goals, and understand the sense of urgency
* Be a self\-motivator with a proactive approach to traditional and unexpected assignments.
* Work well independently and have a collaborative approach to projects
* Demonstrate a proven ability to organize and manage multiple priorities and deadlines
* Have excellent analytical aptitude and a passion for improving organizational processes.
* Show a demonstrated ability to handle confidential information appropriately
* Bring strong interpersonal skills and high emotional intelligence, with the ability to work effectively with employees at all levels
* Operate as a team player with the mindset that it is important to offer help to team members on items both big and small including pressing priority projects
* Approach tasks and requests using a solutions\-oriented framework.
* Have excellent written and verbal communication skills; thoroughness, timeliness, and attention to detail.
* Be flexible and recognize that winning campaigns often means working outside of traditional office hours
* Work well under pressure and handle stress with a sense of humor and grace

**Travel**: The Deputy COO position will require travel, which could be up to 15% of the time. **Salary**: The starting salary for the Deputy COO position is $150,000 on an annualized basis, commensurate with experience and qualifications.  

This is a full\-time, exempt position, that may require work on weekends.  

Due to federal campaign finance rules, only U.S. citizens or U.S. green card holders are eligible for this role. See 52 U.S.C. 30121; 11 C.F.R. 110\.20(i).  

The Democratic National Committee (DNC), is committed to diversity among its staff, and recognizes that its continued success requires the highest commitment to obtaining and retaining a diverse staff that provides the best quality services to supporters and constituents. The DNC is an equal opportunity employer and it is our policy to recruit, hire, train, promote and administer any and all personnel actions without regard to sex, race, age, color, creed, national origin, religion, economic status, sexual orientation, veteran status, gender identity or expression, ethnic identity or physical disability, or any other legally protected basis. The DNC will not tolerate any unlawful discrimination and any such conduct is strictly prohibited. **Benefits:*** The DNC offers a generous benefit package, including:
* Generous paid time off, including federal holidays and open leave
* Health and dental insurance for employee and dependents; 90% paid by the DNC, 10% paid by employee
* Supplementary vision plans available to employees for purchase
* Up to a 5% employer match DNC 401(k) plan
* Pre\-tax flexible spending account benefits available to employees and dependents

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
**Chief Operating Officer (COO) — Taussig Landscape**  
*Wamego / Manhattan, KS \| Full\-Time \| On\-site*

**Lead the Operation. Raise the Standard.**

Taussig Landscape is looking for an experienced operations leader to run a fast\-growing, premium landscape company from the inside out. This isn't a corporate role. It's a hands\-on leadership position for someone who knows how to build teams, drive performance, and run a service business at a high level — every single day.

We're a values\-based company built on the belief that great outdoor spaces change the way people live. Our tagline is *Enriching Lives Through the Outdoors* — and we mean it. If you're someone who takes that kind of pride in what you build, you'll fit right in here.

**What You'll Do**

* Own day\-to\-day operations across all departments — maintenance, install, irrigation, snow, and admin
* Build and enforce the systems, processes, and standards that allow the company to scale without chaos
* Lead, develop, and hold accountable a team of department heads and field leaders
* Drive financial performance — you know your numbers and you manage to them
* Identify and eliminate inefficiencies across production, scheduling, equipment, and workflows
* Own the hiring, onboarding, and training pipeline so we always have the right people in the right seats
* Maintain the culture and standards that make Taussig Landscape a place people want to work
* Partner directly with the owner to translate vision into execution and results

**What We're Looking For**

* Proven experience as a COO, General Manager, or senior operations leader — landscape, construction, or trades background strongly preferred
* Someone who has actually run crews, managed field operations, and understands what it takes to deliver quality work at scale
* A builder — you don't just manage what exists, you improve it
* Strong with people — you can develop leaders, have hard conversations, and build a culture worth being part of
* Data\-driven — you set targets, track the right metrics, and course\-correct fast when something's off
* Organized and disciplined — you run tight meetings, clear processes, and expect the same from your team
* Experience with CRM, project management, or estimating software is a strong plus (LMN, Aspire, or similar)

**What We Offer**

* A seat at the table in a growing, purpose\-driven company with real upside
* Direct partnership with the owner — your decisions will shape the direction of this business
* Competitive compensation based on your experience and the impact you drive
* A team that wants to be led well and a culture that's ready to level up
* The chance to build something worth being proud of

**This role is for someone who wants to lead something real — not manage something comfortable.**

If that's you, send your resume and a short note about your operations background to **Derek@taussiglandscape.com**

*Wamego / Manhattan, KS — Relocation assistance available for the right candidate.*

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $60,000\.00 \- $85,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Employee discount
* Paid time off
* Parental leave
* Professional development assistance
* Relocation assistance
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer, New Orleans Recreation Development
jobs.description:
**POSITION ANNOUNCEMENT**

**New Orleans Recreation Development Commission**

**POSITION: Chief Executive Officer, New Orleans Recreation Development**

**COMMISSION FUNCTION:** The mission of the New Orleans Recreation Development Commission (NORDC) is to advance the physical, mental, and social well\-being of New Orleanians by providing safe and welcoming environments for recreational, athletic, and cultural experiences. The Commission plans, supervises, conducts, and coordinates programs for cultural and physical recreation that will be administered by the New Orleans Recreation Development Commission. NORDC provides a wide range of sports, health, aquatics, day care, and fitness programs for residents and visitors of all ages. NORDC manages senior services, therapeutic recreation, youth camps, teen development, and athletic programs in over 160 facilities city\-wide. The Commission promotes cooperative planning with public and private entities concerned with recreation.

**REQUIREMENTS:**

* Minimum five years of supervisory experience in the administration of a parks and recreation department or sports organization.
* Experience with facilities, operations, and leading and managing professional staff and teams to higher levels of performance.
* Bachelor’s degree or any equivalent combination of education, training, and experience.

**SALARY RANGE**: $150,000 to $200,000

**POSITION SUMMARY**

The CEO of the Commission will have the responsibility to plan, organize, and direct the employees and programs of NORDC; to oversee development, implementation, and administration of NORD policies, programs, and facilities; to manage daily operations; to ensure the effective maintenance and operations of recreation facilities, including all outdoor athletic and recreational centers; to coordinate with and delegate to outside agencies and community groups; to assist in building financial and strategic support for NORDC; and to provide highly effective and comprehensive information and support to the Commission. The CEO receives policy direction from the Commission and works in conjunction with the Executive Director of the NORD Foundation.

**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:**

*Organizational Functions —*

* Serve as an internal consultant to all units and departments/agencies of New Orleans concerning recreation issues and as a key participant in divisional and organizational strategic planning.
* Communicate with the community at large (schools, churches, parent groups) through various channels, including local media, newsletters, annual reports, and speaking to parents' groups, churches, and/or other stakeholders.
* Develop, implement, disseminate, and interpret NORDC policies and procedures.
* Promote teamwork, information\-sharing, and departmental understanding of service goals, objectives, budgeting, and capital improvement projects that support NORDC's mission and strategic plan.
* Oversee the delegation of management and operations of recreation programs for the City of New Orleans.

*Oversight Functions —*

* Visit recreation sites and programs to evaluate program effectiveness and citizen/participant satisfaction.
* Ensure that funding decisions are prioritized according to importance and urgency of needs and that funding actually occurs as directed.
* Prepare reports as directed by the Commission.
* Ensure that programming meets the needs of all city residents, including residents from low\-income backgrounds and those that require special assistance.

*Budgetary/Financial Functions —*

* Review, analyze, and monitor the NORD budget and expenditures.
* Monitor departments to ensure that they are efficiently and effectively providing needed services while staying within budgetary limits.
* Review financial statements, sales and activity reports, and other performance data to measure productivity and goal achievement and to determine areas needing cost reduction and program improvement.
* Direct and coordinate NORDC's financial and budgetary activities in order to fund operations, maximize investments, and increase efficiency.
* Prepare budgets for approval, including those for funding and implementation of programs.
* Negotiate or approve contracts and agreements with suppliers, distributors, federal and state agencies, and other organizational entities.

*Staffing Functions —*

* Establish appropriate service and staffing levels; monitor and evaluate the efficiency and effectiveness of service methods and procedures; allocate resources accordingly; oversee personnel management and negotiation issues.
* Select, train, motivate, and evaluate assigned personnel; provide or coordinate personnel training; work with subordinate personnel to correct deficiencies; and implement discipline and termination procedures.
* Review reports submitted by staff members in order to recommend approval or to suggest changes.

*Community Functions —*

* Respond to and resolve difficult and sensitive citizen inquiries and complaints; review and reply to daily correspondence and phone inquiries.
* Build community support of and engagement with NORDC's mission and strategic plan.
* Develop relationships with individuals and organizations in an effort to financially and strategically support NORDC's mission and strategic plan.
* Represent NORDC to outside agencies and the community; coordinate assigned activities with outside agencies and organizations.

**QUALIFICATIONS:**

*Knowledge of:*

* National best practices of youth development programming
* Business and management: principles involved in strategic planning, resource allocation, human resources modeling, leadership technique, production methods, and coordination of people and resources.
* Principles and practices of marketing and public relations. This includes marketing strategy and tactics, promotion of services provided, and assessments of current and future value of services provided by NORDC.
* Principles and practices of program development and administ

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO)
jobs.description:
Friends of Cyrus, a leading provider agency serving individuals with Intellectual and Developmental Disabilities (IDD), seeks an experienced and hands\-on **Chief Operating Officer** to oversee all daily operations across our residential and day program services. The ideal candidate will be actively engaged in program operations, staff management, and direct service oversight.

Reports directly to the Chief Executive Officer

**Key Responsibilities**

* Oversee all operational aspects of residential and day programs serving adults with IDD (age 21\+)
* Ensure compliance with New Jersey IDD regulations and licensing requirements
* Serve as primary liaison with the New Jersey Division of Developmental Disabilities (DDD)
* Manage all DDD communications, audits, compliance reviews, and regulatory submissions
* Manage program budgets, staffing, and resource allocation
* Supervise Vice Presidents, program directors, supervisors, and direct support professionals
* Conduct regular site visits and quality assurance reviews
* Develop and implement operational policies and procedures
* Ensure person\-centered care and quality of life standards
* Collaborate with CEO on strategic planning and organizational goals
* Address day\-to\-day operational challenges, staff and family concerns
* Maintain regulatory compliance and documentation

**Required Qualifications**

* Master's degree (preference for Business Administration, Public Health, or related field)
* Minimum 10 years in a leadership position within IDD services
* Thorough knowledge of New Jersey regulations and licensing standards
* Demonstrated experience interfacing with the Division of Developmental Disabilities
* Proven experience managing residential and day programs
* Strong operational management skills
* Excellent communication and problem\-solving abilities
* Valid driver's license; willingness to travel between sites

**What We Offer**

* Competitive salary commensurate with experience (200K to 250K)
* Comprehensive benefits package, travel allowance.
* Opportunity to make meaningful impact in the disability services field

**Duties:**

* Develop personal and professional credibility, gain respect, and build trust with leadership within the System particularly with physician leadership. Interact constructively and collaboratively with all colleagues and physicians such that communication and all relationships are strengthened.
* Advises the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Friends of Cyrus II on the organization's operational performance, partnering with Friends of Cyrus II Chief Finance Officer (CFO) for marrying financial and operational performance goals and results.
* Provides support for the strategic planning process and business plan development activities, including accountability for provider and practice onboarding and integration
* In partnership with the Chief Executive Officer of Friends of Cyrus II, develops strategies/alternatives for optimal performance by collaborating with leaders from revenue cycle, managed care, and coding areas.
* Responsible for measuring and improving overall patient satisfaction.
* Working with Human Resource leadership, measure and improve employee satisfaction.
* Thorough understanding of all third\-party payer agreements (including Medicare and medical assistance) including fee for service, pay for quality programs and at\-risk arrangements.
* Accountable for achievement of pay for quality and at\-risk performance standards.
* Ensure the effective management of the resources by recruiting and retaining highly qualified individuals, establishing and monitoring employee development plans and programs for department heads and communicating expectations on a regular basis. Prove goal\-oriented direction to subordinates; assist with problem solving as need arises and notifies individuals promptly when adjustments or problems are perceived.
* Ensure that the highest possible quality of care is delivered to the individuals by continuously monitoring quality assurance plans and procedures and establishing programs to improve quality whenever possible.
* Establish and maintain effecting working relationships with the medical staff, ensuring that their needs and concerns are addressed promptly and effectively.
* Attend promptly to the administrative details of the position, including the signing of documents, preparations of reports, completion of performance appraisals and other employee\-related actions.
* Ensure that all operations of the organization are carried out in an ethical, responsible manner.
* Participates in development of service line operating plans, organizational structure, and objectives for efficient and effective management.
* Participates in recruitment and retention of nonprofessional staff.
* Interprets policies, objectives, and operational procedures.
* Analyzes and recommends changes in organizational systems, policies and procedures and ensures their implementation.
* Participates in the evolution and refinement of population health growth and patient care initiatives.
* Undertakes special projects as directed by the Chief Executive Officer of Friends of Cyrus II.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $180,000\.00 \- $225,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Delta Health is seeking a visionary, collaborative, and mission\-driven **Chief Executive Officer (CEO)** to lead our organization into its next chapter. This is an exceptional opportunity to make a meaningful impact in a rural community while guiding a financially patient\-centered healthcare organization committed to **Excellence, Every Patient, Every Time.**



Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, the CEO serves as the organization's senior executive leader and is responsible for strategic direction, operational excellence, financial stewardship, physician partnerships, workforce engagement, and community relationships.


**What You'll Do**



As CEO, you will:


* Lead the development and execution of the organization's strategic vision.
* Foster a culture focused on quality, safety, accountability, and exceptional patient care.
* Ensure the financial strength and long\-term sustainability of the organization.
* Build strong partnerships with physicians, employees, the Board of Directors, and community stakeholders.
* Champion operational excellence, innovation, and continuous improvement.
* Ensure compliance with all regulatory and accreditation requirements.
* Support leadership development while promoting an engaged and high\-performing workforce.
* Represent Delta Health throughout the community and strengthen regional healthcare partnerships.


**What We're Looking For**



The ideal candidate will bring:


* 7\-10\+ years of rural and independent healthcare leadership experience.
* Previous CEO, COO, or senior executive leadership experience in a hospital or health system preferred.
* Master's degree in Healthcare Administration (MHA), Business Administration (MBA), or related field required.
* Bachelor's degree in Healthcare Administration, Business, or related discipline.
* Demonstrated financial acumen with knowledge of healthcare reimbursement and hospital operations.
* Experience working collaboratively with governing boards, medical staff, and executive leadership.
* Outstanding communication, relationship\-building, and leadership skills.
* Strong strategic thinking, data\-driven decision making, and emotional intelligence.
* FACHE certification preferred.
* ***As a special district it is expected that the candidate will reside in Delta County.***


**Why Delta Health?**



At Delta Health, you'll find more than a leadership opportunity—you'll find a community.



Located on Colorado's beautiful Western Slope, Delta offers an exceptional quality of life with year\-round outdoor recreation, affordable living, outstanding schools, and easy access to Grand Mesa National Forest, Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park, world\-class skiing, hiking, mountain biking, fishing, and hunting.



As our next CEO, you'll have the opportunity to:


* Shape the future of healthcare in a growing rural community.
* Lead an engaged executive team and dedicated workforce.
* Build lasting partnerships with physicians and community leaders.
* Drive innovation while preserving the personalized care that defines rural healthcare.
* Leave a lasting legacy for generations of patients and families.


**Join Our Team**



If you are an authentic leader with a passion for rural healthcare, strategic growth, operational excellence, and community engagement, we encourage you to apply.


**Delta Health is an Equal Opportunity Employer.** We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where every employee can thrive.


Equal Opportunity Employer  

This employer is required to notify all applicants of their rights pursuant to federal employment laws. For further information, please review the Know Your Rights (https://www.eeoc.gov/poster) notice from the Department of Labor.

jobs.title: Head of Global Legal Entity Governance, Managing Director
jobs.description:
**Who we****are****looking for**


We are seeking an expert in legal entity management and subsidiary governance to lead our Global Legal Entity Governance team. The position sits at the intersection of law and regulation, governance, compliance, finance, strategy, technology, risk management, and process optimization.


The ideal candidate will have significant experience building, leading, and evolving a global entity governance function and will have operated within a large, highly regulated institution. This individual will lead multiple direct and indirect team members, partner in the creation, maintenance, dissolution and regulatory compliance activities of various types of legal entities around the globe, and design and implement entity governance frameworks and protocols. The position requires a leader who can translate structural complexity into clear operating principles, drive simplification, and deliver sustainable improvements in processes and procedures. You will report to the Senior Vice President \& Chief Governance Officer.


**Why this role is important to us**


The team you will lead sits within the Office of the Corporate Secretary in State Street’s Legal organization and plays a critical role in enabling the firm to operate as a global, systemically important financial institution. Effective oversight of the firm’s legal entities is foundational to supporting State Street’s strategic priorities, regulatory compliance, and recovery and resolution planning. The position is central to translating structural complexity into clear, scalable governance, driving transparency, strengthening controls, and enabling the firm to respond effectively to regulatory expectations, business change, and growth opportunities.


**What you****will****be responsible for**

As Head of Global Legal Entity Governance, you will build, lead, and evolve the firm’s entity governance capabilities and:


Strategy and Global Legal Entity Governance


* Define and execute the G lobal Legal Entity Governance strategy.
* Align legal entity structures and governance frameworks with State Street’s objectives.
* Advise, as appropriate, on optimal legal entity structures and maintenance requirements.
* Establish and evolve legal entity governance frameworks, policies, and standards.
* Drive consistency and simplification in legal entity management and governance across the enterprise.

Regulatory and Risk Management


* Support compliance with jurisdiction\-specific regulatory, administrative, and governance requirements.
* Identify, assess, and work collaboratively with stakeholders to mitigate legal entity risks, including operational, regulatory, governance, and reputational risks.
* Assess legal entity implications for State Street’s recovery and resolution planning, including divestiture readiness.

Operating Model and Execution


* Design and implement scalable operating models that support full entity lifecycle management (e.g., formation, maintenance, and dissolution).
* Oversee legal entity data management and governance, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and alignment with internal and external reporting requirements.
* Drive adoption of technology\-enabled solutions, including entity management systems, process and workflow tools, and AI use cases.
* Establish clear accountability models across entities and functions.
* Build a high\-performance culture focused on control, execution, and continuous improvement.

Team and Stakeholder Management


* Manage a team of legal entity professionals directly and , indirectly, a network of entity secretaries that support State Street’s legal entities.
* Partner with senior stakeholders across Legal, Risk, Compliance, Finance, and the business units.
* Provide executive\-quality reporting on legal entity matters.
* Interface, as needed, with regulators, auditors, and other stakeholders.

**What we value**

The se skills will help you s ucceed in this role:


* Subject \- matter expertise : a deep understanding of global legal entity management, regulatory expectations, governance frameworks, and structural risk management ;
* Strategic leadership and execution : an ability to define a clear vision and strategy and translate that strategy into a scalable, disciplined operating model ;
* Transformation mindset : a bias toward simplification, efficiency, and continuous improvement, including the use of technology and data to modernize processes;
* Analytical rigor and problem solving : an ability to assess complex issues, apply sound judgment , and drive effective risk\-informed decisions;
* Executive presence and communication : an ability to inspire confidence in others , build credibility, and communicate with clarity and precision ;
* Leadership and influence : demonstrated success building, leading, and developing high\-performing teams, while also influencing outcomes across a matrixed organization without direct authority ; and
* Judgment in the face of complexity and ambiguity : an ability to operate effectively in a complex global organization, make decisions with incomplete information, and balance competing priorities and perspectives.

**Required****Qualifications**


* Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree (e.g., JD, MBA, or equivalent) is a plus.
* 1 5 \+ years directly relevant experience in l egal entity management/ subsidiary governance ; experience within a global financial services organization is a plus.
* Working knowledge of Diligent Entities legal entity management platform and similar tools.

$170,000 \- $282,500 Annual
The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

jobs.title: Chief Philanthropy Officer
jobs.description:
**Reports to:** President/CEO

**ORGANIZATION PROFILE:**

Historic Charleston Foundation (HCF) is a non\-profit organization advancing the broad mission of historic preservation and protecting the livability of the community. As historic preservation has evolved into more than a movement to simply save historic buildings, the Foundation has broadened its scope into balancing the needs of modern society with protecting the significant cultural fabric of our historic communities. The Foundation’s three main mission pillars are Preservation, Advocacy, and Museums.

**POSITION SUMMARY:**

The Chief Philanthropy Officer (CPO) is accountable for the overall management and success of a well\-rounded Philanthropy Department that addresses all private fundraising (annual, major, planned), fundraising related events and sponsorships, grants, and volunteer engagement. The successful candidate will collaborate with the CEO, executive team, volunteer committees, and the Board of Trustees to plan and execute resource development strategies and measure performance against goals. The ideal candidate will partner with colleagues, community groups, and other stakeholders, lead complex projects, and serve as the organization’s champion among key donor constituencies. The Chief Philanthropy Officer reports directly to the President \& CEO.

**JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:**

**Leadership**

* Lead, mentor, and supervise a Philanthropy team of three additional full\-time professionals
* Collaborate with the CEO, other Department Heads, and Board of Trustees in all philanthropic activities to fund the organization’s operations and deliver programs within the community
* Create and manage an effective annual plan for a fully realized Philanthropy Department that supports the organization’s three mission areas. Execute an objective, measurable plan in coordination with the HCF team, consultants, and volunteers
* Collaborate with the Foundation’s marketing and communications team as well as external marketing partners

**Revenue Generation**

* Lead all efforts to meet and/or exceed annual revenue goals as determined by strategic planning efforts, organizational needs, and proper assessment of philanthropic readiness
* Directly manage and advance a major gift portfolio
* Oversee efforts to create, execute, and evaluate key events directly impacting the Philanthropy Department’s fundraising goals to cultivate and steward leadership donors to HCF and generate operating revenue
* Direct the overall donor and prospect pipeline to ensure ongoing acquisition, retention, growth

**Revenue Forecasting**

* Develop and maintain monthly and annual revenue forecasts
* Develop and implement an annual resource development plan that includes long\- and short\-term objectives for the philanthropy department with measurable revenue targets
* Participate in the development, implementation, and monitoring of the organization’s philanthropic development activities, through controlling expenditures within budget and maintaining donor and financial records

**Community Engagement**

* Identify, recruit, and retain volunteers to drive engagement, solicitation, and stewardship in each mission area
* Lead, support, and collaborate with the Philanthropy Committee of the Board of Trustees
* Oversee the management of HCF giving societies, including the Granville Bastion, the 1947 Society, and PreserVISIONists
* Develop strategic alliances with community leaders and public officials
* Work with the Marketing Department to ensure philanthropy messaging is present in efforts to increase visibility of the organization in the community and foster good public relations with our partners and stakeholders

**Development Operations**

* Create and manage a team to maximize the current constituent relationship management system (CRM) to identify specific giving opportunities and guide strategies across the donor pipeline to increase giving and grow key relationships

**Chief Executive Officer \| Occlunova**
========================================


**Location:** USA, with a preference for Minnesota or New Jersey, with regular travel to clinical sites and occasional travel to NLC Health Ventures in Amsterdam


**Commitment:** Full\-time


**Compensation:** Meaningful equity participation \+ competitive compensation


**Reports to:** Board of Directors


**About the Venture – Occlunova**
---------------------------------



Occlunova is a medtech venture developing the next generation of Left Atrial Appendage Occlusion (LAAO) technology to prevent stroke in patients with atrial fibrillation (AF). AF affects more than 60 million people worldwide and drives a 5x increase in stroke risk. Yet, existing LAAO devices remain limited by leak rates, traumatic anchoring mechanisms, and persistent device\-related thrombosis.



Occlunova's platform combines an anatomy\-conforming balloon with an atraumatic anchoring method engineered to reduce leaks, minimize procedural risk, and let AF patients safely come off oral anticoagulant therapy. The company is also integrating left atrial appendage electrical isolation (LAAEI) into the same device, enabling occlusion and ablation to be performed in a single procedure \- an increasingly relevant combination with no direct equivalent on the market today.



The technology has been validated in benchtop, ex\-vivo (human cadaver), and in\-vivo (canine) studies that indicate measurable advantages in comparison with market technologies.



Occlunova has raised its pre\-seed and is preparing for seed financing to fund its first\-in\-human studies in 2027\. All major structural heart strategics are active acquirers in this space, and pre\-approval exits are the norm rather than the exception.



Occlunova is part of the venture portfolio of NLC Health Ventures, built in collaboration with Fusion MedTech and clinical collaborators at Mayo Clinic.


**The Opportunity**
-------------------



We are looking for an experienced medtech CEO to take Occlunova from a validated prototype with a strong clinical and technical team in place through further clinical development and towards a strategic exit. This is a company\-building role for a leader who has done this before: someone who has taken an implantable cardiovascular device through fundraising, clinical trials, and ideally into the hands of a strategic acquirer or to market.



You will own the company's overall strategy and be the primary face of Occlunova to investors, clinical partners, and future acquirers, while building the team and organization needed to execute against an ambitious but realistic timeline.


**Your Role**
-------------


### **Set Strategy and Lead the Company**


* Own overall corporate strategy, prioritization, and resource allocation across product, clinical, and regulatory workstreams
* Translate the LAAO/LAAEI platform into a clear, milestone\-driven path to value creation, together with identifying and managing key partners like CROs, CDMOs, advisors, and other key suppliers.
* Represent Occlunova externally to investors, KOLs, strategics, and industry partners

### **Lead Fundraising**


* Drive seed and subsequent financing rounds, including investor targeting, materials, diligence, and negotiation
* Build and maintain relationships with venture investors, strategic corporate partners, and non\-dilutive funding sources
* Manage capital efficiently against the milestones that matter most for the next raise and eventual exit

### **Advance Clinical Development**


* Own the design, sequencing, and execution of clinical studies from first\-in\-human through pivotal trial
* Build relationships with interventional cardiologists, electrophysiologists, and KOLs in structural heart and AF ablation
* Work with regulatory advisors to shape a credible pathway through FDA (and OUS regulators as relevant)

### **Build Toward a Strategic Outcome**


* Understand the competitive and M\&A landscape in LAAO and PFA ablation, and position Occlunova accordingly
* Build and sustain relationships with corporate development teams at strategic acquirers
* Prepare the company operationally and financially for partnership or exit conversations

### **Build and Lead the Organization**


* Recruit and lead a high\-performing team across clinical, regulatory, engineering, and operations
* Establish the organizational structure, culture, and processes needed to scale from a small team to a clinical\- and subsequently commercial\-stage company
* Foster a culture of creativity, rigor, and perseverance, and urgency appropriate to a capital\-efficient medtech startup

**Your Profile**
----------------



We are looking for a CEO who combines deep medtech operating experience with the strategic thinking and creativity needed to build a company from this stage forward.


### **Preferred Background**


* Experience in implantable medical devices, ideally in cardiovascular or structural heart
* A track record of successfully raising capital for a medtech venture (venture capital, strategic, and/or non\-dilutive), including the financial acumen required to forecast and manage
* Experience organizing and managing clinical trials for an implantable device, from early feasibility through pivotal study
* Strong team\-building and organizational skills, with the ability to recruit and lead a multidisciplinary team

**ABOUT TGWA**

The Texas Ground Water Association is a 501(c)(6\) association for the Texas water well drilling industry. TGWA works to promote the industry and facilitate cooperation and educational opportunities among water well drillers, pump installers, manufacturers, suppliers, geoscientists, and government and scientific agencies. The organization has more than 2,500 individual members and undertakes an annual TGWA Convention each January. It is also a leading provider of in\-person and online continuing education classes for water well drillers and pump installers in the state.

This full\-time staff person will serve as the chief executive and financial officer for TGWA and the TGWA Scholarship Foundation (TGWA SF), a separate 501(c)(3\) organization dedicated to providing scholarships for Texas undergraduate students. The position reports directly to the TGWA and TGWA SF boards of directors and requires extensive contact with TGWA members, industry experts, Texas Department of Licensing \& Regulation (TDLR) staff, and National Ground Water Association (NGWA) personnel. Some travel (up to 35%) is also required, as the position includes participation at multiple association events across the state.

**IDEAL CANDIDATE**

The Executive Director should be an organized self\-starter with association management experience and legislative work experience The director will help TGWA grow membership and develop a strong presence in water policy discussions at the Texas Legislature. The candidate should be committed to managing staff, developing strong relationships with TGWA board members, and strengthening and expanding benefits for association members.

**ESSENTIAL RESPONSIBILITIES**

**Organization Overview**

The original Freedom Quilting Bee (Bee), was a women’s quilting cooperative established in 1966 during the tumultuous times of the Civil Rights Movement. The cooperative manufactured quilts for the largest retailers in America; Sears, Bloomingdale’s and interior designers in New York City.

***Our Mission \- To teach, share and preserve threads of quilting arts, resistance \& legacy of Black women in Gee’s Bend and Alberta, Alabama.***

***Our Vision \- To inspire makers across the globe, sharing threads of transformative art, culture, and the pioneering legacy of Black women in Gee’s Bend and Alberta, Alabama.*** 

Freedom Quilting Bee Legacy (Legacy) was founded to ensure the heroic, historic, innovative, artistic and incredible odds\-beating work of the women of the original Bee isn’t lost to history. We celebrate and teach about the work and accomplishments of those women (and men) past and present who made the Bee such a powerful artistic and community force. We will offer real economic opportunities for a new generation of quilt makers and artisans.

We provide solutions to community needs, such as Internet access, adult learning and skills building.

We draw tourism to the area and offer a deeper experience of the history and current activities, with shops, lodging, classes and interactive exhibits.

We preserve the past and evolve into the future by creating jobs, building wealth and teaching life skills to improve our community and its people..

Posting Details
  
  

THIS POSTING WILL BE USED FOR ONGOING RECRUITMENT AND MAY CLOSE AT ANY TIME. APPLICANT LISTS MAY BE SCREENED MORE THAN ONCE.
  
  

Please visit us at moifa.org
  
  

Created in 1978 by the New Mexico Legislature, the Department of Cultural Affairs represents New Mexico's dedication to preserving and celebrating the cultural integrity and diversity of our state. The Department oversees a broad range of New Mexico's arts and cultural heritage agencies. These include 15 divisions representing a variety of programs and services.
  
  

The Museum of International Folk Art (MOIFA) shapes a humane world by connecting people through creative expression and artistic traditions. The Museum's holdings represent diverse cultures and constitute the largest collection of international folk art in the world. The core collection donated by Museum founder Florence Dibell Bartlett and representing 34 countries has grown to a collection of over 163,000 objects from more than 130 countries. MOIFA offers dynamic exhibitions, programs, and publications that appeal to diverse learning styles and audiences, touch the heart, engage the mind, and inspire action. We create accessible exhibitions, programming, and resources that serve the global community, including multiple perspectives that encourage further exploration of wide\-ranging subjects.
  
Why does the job exist?
  
  

As the chief operating officer, the deputy director oversees all operations at the MOIFA, including human, financial, and physical resources. Daily oversight of Museum operations by the deputy director directly impacts all facets of the Museum's mission. Responsibilities span a wide range of activities, including strategic planning, budgeting preparation, preservation of and access to collections, visitor services, staff personnel policies, and financial backup and management of facilities maintenance and repairs. The deputy director represents the Museum in the executive director's absence and as delegated by the executive director with all constituencies affiliated with the Museum.
  
How does it get done?
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
  

Museum Operations and Administration
  
  

* Assists the Executive Director in the overall administration and daily operations of the Museum.
* Oversees facilities operations, security, emergency preparedness, health and safety, and building management.
* Supervises assigned staff; develops work plans, evaluates performance, and ensures compliance with State Personnel Rules.
* Leads operational planning, policy development, and cross\-divisional projects.
* Serves as the Museum's owner representative for capital, construction, facilities, exhibition, collections, and security projects.
* Assists with strategic planning, capital improvement initiatives, and operational resource management.


Executive Leadership Support
  
  

* Serves as principal backup to the Executive Director, ensuring continuity of leadership and operations.
* Represents the Museum with Department of Cultural Affairs leadership, governing boards, partner organizations, donors, and the public.
* Assists with strategic initiatives, special projects, contracts, and executive\-level decision making.
* Coordinates with the Executive Director to ensure alignment with organizational priorities and strategic goals.


Fiscal, Human Resources, and Administrative Management
  
  

* Assists with budget development, financial oversight, and fiscal administration.

* Oversees grant administration, compliance, reporting, and financial coordination.
* Develops and improves administrative procedures, financial protocols, and operational processes.
* Monitors expenditures and resource utilization, ensuring compliance with state policies and organizational objectives.
* Supports conflict resolution, organizational effectiveness, and continuous improvement initiatives.


Who are the customers?
  
  

This position serves Museum visitors, Museum staff, members, supporters, partners, friends, citizens of the state of New Mexico, with particular attention to underserved communities within our state, to help them connect with folk art traditions, including their own, as well as our national and international community of artists and institutional collaborators.
  
Ideal Candidate
  
  

This position serves Museum visitors, Museum staff, members, supporters, partners, friends, citizens of the state of New Mexico, with particular attention to underserved communities within our state, to help them connect with folk art traditions, including their own, as well as our national and international community of artists and institutional collaborators.
  
Minimum Qualification
  
  

Bachelor's Degree in a relevant field of study from an accredited college or university and nine (9\) years of professional level experience operating within parameters and guidelines directly related to the purpose of the position defined by the agency at the time of recruitment. Any combination of education from an accredited college or university and/or direct experience in this occupation totaling thirteen (13\) years may substitute for the required education and experience. A hiring agency will designate a portion of the required experience to include supervisory and/or specialized experience. Any required licensure, certification or registration shall be defined at the time of recruitment and will be in addition to the above requirements.
  
Employment Requirements
  
  

Must possess and maintain a current Defensive Driving Course Certificate from the State of New Mexico or must pass and receive Defensive Driving Course Certification as a condition of continued employment. Must possess and maintain a valid Driver's License.
  
Working Conditions
  
  

Work is performed in an office setting within a public museum with exposure to V

Compensation: Competitive compensation based on experience.
  

Classification: Full\-time, salaried exempt
  

This role requires flexibility, including occasional evening and weekend work for events, donor
  

engagement, artist relations, board meetings, installation, de\-installation, and other mission critical activities
  

Reports to: Board of Directors
  

Supervises: N/A  



  

**Organizational Overview**  

SculptureWalk Sioux Falls is a 501(c)(3\) and is one of the largest annual exhibits of public
  

sculptures in the country. Our mission is to bring art to the people, and our guiding principle is to
  

be the highest quality, most professional, financially strong, artist friendly, year\-round outdoor
  

sculpture program in the United States. Curated by a blind jury of local dignitaries and art
  

enthusiasts, works are graciously loaned by the artists and installed in the spring through
  

downtown Sioux Falls, SD, to be continuously displayed all year.  



  

**Position Summary**  

The Executive Director (ED) is the chief executive and fundraiser for SculptureWalk Sioux Falls,
  

responsible for the organization’s leadership, financial sustainability, and long\-term success.
  

The ED is responsible for leading daily operations, advancing the strategic plan, achieving
  

organizational goals, and ensuring the organization has the resources, relationships, and
  

operating discipline needed to fulfill its mission.

communication, regular reporting, and appropriate respect for the distinction between board

practices, sponsorship strategy, and grant opportunities.

and event logistics, then recommend improvements.

* Create a year\-round engagement calendar that supports fundraising, public awareness,

donor cultivation, artist relationships, and community participation. **Essential Duties and Responsibilities**

* Serve as the organization’s chief executive, accountable for overall performance,

practices.
  

* Work with the finance firm, Treasurer, Finance Committee, and Board to support

budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, audits or reviews, internal controls, and longterm financial planning.
  

* Provide timely, accurate, and transparent financial information to the Board, including

financial performance, risks, opportunities, and recommended corrective action when
  

needed.

events.

**Board Relations \& Board Development**

jobs.title: Director of Development, College of Business and College of Engineering
jobs.description:
*Be a trusted, strategic, fundraising partner to enable the University of Nebraska to change lives and save lives.*

* Do you have the drive and desire to partner with professionals and leaders who are a force for good in Nebraska and beyond?
* Have you been a successful fundraiser, well\-versed in the best practices of the profession?
* Do you have the vision and executive presence to help alumni and philanthropic partners fulfill their legacies?
* Do you have a curiosity and appreciation for the University of Nebraska and a strong desire to support the next generation of leaders?


If so, we invite you to apply for this exciting role!


At the University of Nebraska Foundation, we grow relationships and resources that enable the University of Nebraska to change lives and save lives. We believe that our strong commitment to inclusion and respect is essential to accomplishing this mission through our values of teamwork, integrity, and passion for mission. We invite, explore, and celebrate differences to increase productivity, spark innovation and most importantly, accomplish our mission.



You will join the foundation at an exciting time, with the opportunity to make a transformational impact. The foundation is in the public phase of a historic campaign to raise $3 billion to build a better future for Nebraska. The campaign is being led by many enthusiastic volunteers representing all areas of the University of Nebraska System. UNL anchors the campaign, with a goal of $1\.5 billion from enthusiastic donors. ***These positions will support the College of Business or College of Engineering and will be based in our Lincoln office.***


**Ways you will make an impact:**

* Play a critical role in building a robust community of philanthropic support by partnering with university staff and college administrators, leadership, or faculty.
* Plan and implement major gift fundraising strategies, including identification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of major gift donors.
* Foster relationships with alumni, community leaders, and engaged philanthropists to align their philanthropy with the charitable priorities of the colleges.
* Assist in the creation and implementation of the designated campus’ overall development strategy.


**Who we want:**

* **Relationally talented individuals**who make authentic connections, facilitate meaningful conversations, and provide customized solutions to help generous donors fulfill their philanthropic goals.
* **Energetic learners** who are humble, inquisitive, and open\-minded.
* **Self\-motivated and driven**individuals who want to make a difference and are results driven.
* **Collaborative and positive** professionals who can facilitate meaningful conversations with a wide variety of people and are passionate about what they do.
* **Compassionate communicators** who are energized by the opportunity to build long lasting partnerships with others.


**What you need:**

* **Minimum Education:** Bachelor’s degree required. **Preferred Education**: Master’s degree.
* **Experience**: 1\-3 years’ experience in development, fundraising, sales, or a related field with transferrable skills.
* Demonstrated success in the solicitation of annual and major gifts, or transferable skills.
* Ability to coordinate and manage a variety of projects and programs simultaneously.
* Discretion in handling and navigating confidential matters.
* Demonstrated strong oral and written communication skills.
* Candidates will also be evaluated on our core values of passion for mission, integrity, and teamwork.


**What we offer:**

* ***This position will work in our Lincoln office. We offer flexibility for hybrid work******. This position will require travel.***
* Mission\-driven work that changes lives and saves lives.
* A strengths\-based, engagement\-focused, and performance\-oriented culture.
* Ongoing learning and development opportunities for your career growth.
* An award\-winning wellness program.
* 12 weeks of paid medical leave, including maternity and paternity leave.
* 12 paid holidays and generous vacation time.
* A generous retirement match and multiple incentive compensation programs.
* University of Nebraska tuition reimbursement for you and your dependents.


**Who we are:**


* Recognition by the Chronicle of Philanthropy as one of America’s Favorite Charities.
* Founded in 1936, the University of Nebraska Foundation provides support for the campuses in the university system — the University of Nebraska–Lincoln, University of Nebraska at Kearney, University of Nebraska at Omaha, Nebraska College of Technical Agriculture in Curtis, and the University of Nebraska Medical Center and its primary clinical partner, Nebraska Medicine.
* Among public universities, the University of Nebraska ranks in the top 25 nationally for both funds raised and endowed assets under management.
* The foundation has a reputation for efficiency and effectiveness, raising more than $7 for every $1 spent.
* Winner of Gallup’s Don Clifton Strengths\-Based Culture Award.
* Winner of the Governor’s Harvester Wellness Award, the highest honor for corporate wellness.


*The University of Nebraska Foundation is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to a work environment free from discrimination and harassment where all employees feel respected and valued. We will consider all qualified applicants in accordance with applicable law.*


Equal Opportunity Employer  

This employer is required to notify all applicants of their rights pursuant to federal employment laws. For further information, please review the Know Your Rights (https://www.eeoc.gov/poster) notice from the Department of Labor.

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
**Be part of a values\-driven Life Plan Community that has set standards of excellence in retirement living since 1967!**

Foulkeways at Gwynedd was the first Continuing Care Retirement Community (CCRC) in Pennsylvania and the first Quaker CCRC in the United States when we opened our doors in 1967\. With over 50\-years serving the aging population, we continue to reflect the Quaker ideals of friendship, dignity and respect for all our residents and diverse staff.

We are currently searching for an enthusiastic, knowledgeable and detail\-oriented **Director of Development** to join our leadership team.

***Note: This position will be a Part\-Time position. The exact number of hours per week is flexible.***

As the Director of Development for the community, this position has the responsibility to plan, develop, organize, implement, evaluate and direct all philanthropic efforts and programs within the context of Foulkeways mission and philosophies. The Director of Development will develop Foulkeways’ philanthropic vision and will promote a culture of philanthropy among Foulkeways residents, their families, our board and staff.

The Director of Development will cultivate relationships with all stakeholders, especially residents, to encourage and celebrate gift giving to promote Foulkeways’ short\-term and long\-term goals. In addition, the Director of Development will appeal to outside foundations, vendors and the greater community to keep Foulkeways at the forefront of new ideas, technology and projects.

**Responsibilities** of the Director of Development are as follows (these are not inclusive of all job responsibilities):

* Develop philanthropy program, as well as communications plan and supporting materials
* Cultivate positive relationships with company stakeholders
* Develop and maintain lists of potential donors
* Manage the receipt and administration of deferred gifts, such as bequests, gift annuities and trusts
* Report to the Board and Fund Development Committee
* Present Development\-related information to stakeholders through committee participation, group presentations, internals publications, etc.
* Complete administrative tasks as needed

The Director of Development of Foulkeways will promote the mission and values of Foulkeways and will assist in the creation of a culture that celebrates diversity, equity, inclusion and belonging.

**Qualifications Required:**

* Bachelor’s degree in a related field required
* Possess thorough knowledge of charitable giving tax law
* Minimum 5 years prior development experience in a health care setting

**Qualifications Preferred:**

* Certified Fund\-Raising Executive (CFRE) certification preferred
* Excellent computer skills with fundraising software experience preferred

***Do you think you are the perfect fit for this position? If so, please apply today!***

To learn more about Foulkeways, go to https://www.foulkeways.org/

The health and safety of our team members and residents is our top priority. Therefore, we do require that all employees of Foulkeways be vaccinated against Influenza. Exemptions based on religious beliefs and qualifying medical reasons will be considered.

Foulkeways operates on the principle of equal opportunity for all qualified persons regardless of race, color, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, national origin, disability, or any other characteristic protected by law in evaluating any person for employment, training, compensation, promotion, or termination. We operate in accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act. **EOE/ADA**

Work Location: In person

**Annual Salary:** $145,000 \- $160,000

  


**Overview**:



The Senior Director of Development serves as Heading Home's lead frontline fundraiser, responsible for building meaningful relationships with individual philanthropists who are passionate about preventing and ending homelessness. This position works closely with the CEO to develop and execute strategies to identify, cultivate, solicit and steward major donors whose investments advance Heading Home's mission and long\-term strategic priorities.



This role requires a highly relationship\-driven fundraising professional with a proven ability to secure transformational gifts, develop new philanthropic partnerships, and inspire donors through compelling storytelling and measurable impact. Working closely with the CEO, Board members, and Development team, the Senior Director of Development will develop a plan to expand Heading Home's major gifts program while strengthening relationships with our most significant supporters.

* Maintain an active pipeline in qualified opportunities to assist in getting to the fundraising goal.
* Build and actively manage a portfolio of major gift prospects and donors.
* Develop individualized cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship strategies for each assigned donor.
* Conduct discovery meetings to identify donor interests, philanthropic priorities, and giving capacity.
* Personally solicit and close five\-, six\-, and seven\-figure gifts.
* Maintain regular communication with assigned donors through meetings, calls, correspondence, and program updates.

  


**Business Development**


* Identify, qualify, and cultivate new major gift prospects throughout Massachusetts and beyond.

Set team goals, monitor performance, and ensure accountability for fundraising and engagement objectives.

Normal for an office work environment.

jobs.title: Program Director III - Heartland Housing Sanctuary
jobs.description:
At Heartland Family Service, we are committed to building a culture that is trauma informed and values and celebrates diversity and inclusion. We believe this allows for better service delivery and innovation, as it encourages our employees to bring various experiences and uniqueness to the workplace.



Trauma Informed Care is an approach to engaging people with histories of trauma that recognizes the presence of trauma symptoms and acknowledges the role that trauma has played in their lives. Trauma Informed Care also acknowledges the effects of working with trauma survivors on our workforce and seeks to build collaborative and supportive working environments and relationships.



If you believe in our mission of creating healthy families and communities, and like a fast pace, collaborative, and team\-oriented environment, then Heartland Family Service is the agency for you.


**SUMMARY DESCRIPTION OF WORK**  

  

This position will work as part of the Sanctuary House team working in close collaboration with the domestic violence sexual assault program service team, all under the umbrella of survivor services. The program provides services designed to meet needs related to immediate safety, trauma recovery, and on\-going support for survivors of human trafficking. This position is responsible for direct oversight of the crisis stabilization shelter, advocacy, and community based programming to include housing services.


**Compensation:** Salary is determined by total years of relevant experience  

**Work Schedule:** 40 hour work week


**Click to see benefits and company perks (https://www.heartlandfamilyservice.org/careers/)**


**MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS**

  

* Bachelors Degree, Masters preferred
* 2 years experience in social services or related field; of which a minimum of 1 year experience providing domestic violence, sexual assault, or trafficking services
* Management experience preferred
* Valid Drivers License and acceptable driving record


**Essential Duties and Responsibilities**

  

* + Foster an empowered, values\-driven team culture that reflects HFS's mission and agency values, encouraging staff development, growth, and collaborative problem\-solving.
* + Provide regular supervision and support for all assigned program staff.
* \*Assist with recruitment to include posting openings, screening applicants, scheduling \*interviews, and checking references
* + Hire, supervise monitor, train, evaluate and develop direct care program staff.
* + Ensure 24/7 coverage of crisis stabilization shelter and on\-call crisis response advocate programs.
* + Participate in an on\-call rotation to provide crisis support and guidance to program staff outside of regular business hours.
* + Assist with service delivery and project implementation throughout the program.

Windsor Locks, CT 06096  

United States  




Salary: $220,000 \- 250,000



The New England Air Museum (NEAM) seeks an accomplished leader with demonstrated success in fundraising, strategic leadership, and organizational management to serve as its next Executive Director. One of the nation's premier aviation museums and the largest aerospace museum in New England, NEAM preserves and presents the remarkable story of flight while inspiring future generations through immersive exhibitions, nationally recognized aircraft restoration, engaging public programs, and hands\-on STEM education. Home to more than 100 historic aircraft and aerospace artifac ts ranging from early flying machines to supersonic jets and spacecraft, the Museum occupies a unique place at the intersection of history, science, technology, and innovation.  

  

The New England Air Museum  

Founded in 1959, the New England Air Museum (NEAM) is one of the nation's premier aviation museums. Located adjacent to Bradley International Airport in Windsor Locks, Connecticut, the Museum preserves and presents the remarkable story of flight while inspiring future generations through immersive exhibitions, nationally recognized aircraft restoration, hands\-on STEM education, and engaging public programs.
  

  

The Museum's 150,000\-square\-foot campus includes three expansive exhibit hangars, restoration facilities, educational spaces, a research library, and administrative offices. Its collection encompasses more than 100 historic aircraft and aerospace vehicles, along with thousands of engines, artifacts, photographs, archival materials, and related objects documenting the evolution of flight from the earliest experiments through the Space Age. The collection is interpreted through thematic exhibitions exploring military aviation, commercial flight, helicopters, New England's aerospace heritage, jet propulsion, and space exploration. Throughout the galleries, visitors encounter iconic aircraft alongside engaging stories of the pilots, engineers, designers, astronauts, and innovators whose vision transformed aviation and continues to shape our world.
  

  

NEAM refreshes the visitor experience through new exhibitions and interpretive initiatives that connect history with contemporary issues and emerging technologies. Recent projects have explored subjects including the area's pioneering contributions to jet propulsion, the untold stories of the Tuskegee Airmen, Women in Aviation, and New England's role in space exploration. Looking ahead, major planned exhibition initiatives will explore the history of balloons , blimps, and airships; and Connecticut's contributions to the Space Race with expanded digital interpretation to reflect the Museum's commitment to presenting aviation history in innovative and engaging ways.
  

  

One of the Museum's defining characteristics is its nationally respected aircraft restoration program. Supported by an extraordinary corps of skilled volunteers working alongside professional staff, restoration projects preserve historically significant aircraft using meticulous research and craftsmanship. Visitors are able to observe restoration work in progress, providing an authentic behind\-the\-scenes experience while showcasing the expertise and dedication required to preserve these remarkable artifacts for future generations.
  

  

Education lies at the heart of NEAM's mission. Through school field trips, summer camps, merit badge workshops, flight simulators, family programs, lectures, and STEM\-focused educational experiences, the Museum serves more than 5,000 students annually while introducing young people to careers in science, engineering, aviation, and advanced manufacturing. Working in partnership with area schools, colleges, universities, and aerospace companies, NEAM is increasingly serving as a bridge between the region's remarkable aviation heritage and its future workforce, inspiring the next generation of engineers, technicians, pilots, and innovators.
  

  

The Museum also presents a vibrant year\-round calendar of public programs and special events that encourage repeat visitation and broaden community engagement. Signature programs including Women Take Flight, Space Expo, and Open Cockpit Weekends welcome families, aviation enthusiasts, educators, veterans, and lifelong learners.  

  

NEAM is situated in the heart of one of America's historic aerospace corridors, occupying a unique position within a region long associated with aviation innovation and advanced manufacturing. Connect icut is home to globally recognized aerospace companies including Pratt \& Whitney, Sikorsky, Collins Aerospace, and numerous suppliers whose engineering achievements have shaped aviation and space exploration for generations. This setting provides the Museum with exceptional opportunities to collaborate with industry leaders, educational institutions, veterans' organizations, and community partners.  

  

NEAM welcomes approximately 75,000 visitors annually and is supported by a dedicated professional staff of approximately 40 employees, an engaged Board of Directors, and an exceptional volunteer corps that contributes thousands of hours each year across restoration, collections, education, visitor services, and special events. The Museum operates with annual revenues of approximately $3\.5 million and a $2 million board\-designated endowment.
  

  

The Opportunity  

  

The New England Air Museum has made significant strides in strengthening its exhibitions, e ducational programming, marketing, and public visibility over the past several years.
