jobs.title: Chief Advancement & Executive Director of the College Foundation
jobs.description:
The Chief Advancement Officer and Executive Director of the College Foundation serves as the senior advancement executive for the College and provides leadership for all philanthropic and foundation\-related activities. This role plans, organizes, and manages a comprehensive development program, including major and planned gifts, annual giving, corporate and foundation relations, donor stewardship, and advancement operations. Approximately 75% of the role is dedicated to college advancement functions, with up to 25% devoted to leadership and management of the College Foundation. The Foundation is a legally separate 501(c)(3\) organization established to support institutional priorities and student success; the Executive Director oversees the Foundation’s assets and provides reporting as required of 501(c)(3\) organizations in the Commonwealth of Massachusetts.  

  

The Chief Advancement Officer works closely with the President, the Foundation Board of Directors, senior leadership, and external stakeholders to advance the College's mission, strengthen community partnerships, and ensure ethical, effective, and compliant fundraising practices. The Chief Advancement Officer will supervise the Senior Director of Operations and Donor Services and Associate Director of Stewardship, Donor Engagement and Operations.


**Key Responsibilities:**


Advancement and Fundraising Leadership


* Identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward individual, corporate, and foundation donors in support of College priorities
* Develop and execute an annual strategic and operational advancement plan with measurable revenue goals
* Serve as the principal philanthropic contact for internal and external constituents, including senior leadership, donors, and community partners
* Liaise with academic and administrative departments to develop funding opportunities and donor impact reports
* Manage a personal portfolio of major and planned gift prospects while providing strategic oversight of the College's overall donor pipeline

Foundation Leadership and Governance


* Serve as Executive Director of the College Foundation, providing leadership and administrative oversight for a legally separate 501(c)(3\) organization established to support the mission and priorities of the College
* Act as the primary staff liaison to the Foundation Board of Directors, supporting the Board in fulfilling its fiduciary responsibilities while respecting its independent governance authority
* Ensure close alignment between College priorities and Foundation fundraising activities, in coordination with and under the direction of the President of the College
* Oversee Foundation financial and operational functions, including budgeting, audits, tax filings, investments, and compliance with applicable state and federal requirements
* Accept and administer gifts on behalf of the Foundation in accordance with donor intent, Foundation policies, and College priorities

Management and Operations


* Direct all aspects of the Advancement Office, including supervision of the Senior Director of Operations and Donor Services and Associate Director of Stewardship, Donor Engagement and Operations
* Establish and maintain effective advancement infrastructure, systems, and reporting processes
* Use advancement data and performance metrics to assess progress, inform strategy, and improve fundraising effectiveness
* Prepare regular reports on fundraising performance and Foundation initiatives for College leadership and the Foundation Board

College\-Wide Competencies and Expectations


* Ability to advance philanthropic strategies that support student success and access, consistent with the community college mission
* Strong collaborative and relationship\-building skills
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* High standards of integrity, ethical judgment, and accountability

Supervision


* Supervision Received: President of the College
* Supervision Exercised:
* Senior Director, Operations and Donor Services
* Associate Director, Stewardship, Donor Engagement and Operations


**Requirements:**


Education


* Bachelor's degree required from an accredited institution
* Master's degree preferred.


Foundation \& Governance


* Demonstrated experience working with or supporting a nonprofit governing board, with an understanding of fiduciary responsibilities, ethical fundraising practices, and donor intent
* Working knowledge of nonprofit operations and compliance relevant to a 501(c)(3\) organization, including gift acceptance, financial oversight, and regulatory requirements

Executive Leadership \& Management


* Proven ability to lead and manage professional staff, set strategic direction, and establish effective systems, processes, and performance metrics
* Demonstrated capacity to serve as a senior institutional leader reporting to a president or chief executive, exercising sound judgment, discretion, and accountability
* Demonstrated senior level leadership in comprehensive advancement or development programs, including major gifts, planned giving, annual giving, corporate and foundation relations, and donor stewardship

Communication \& Professional Standards


* Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to represent the institution and its foundation with clarity, credibility, and confidence
* Demonstrated commitment to integrity, ethical judgment, and accountability in advancement and nonprofit leadership

**Preferred Requirements:**


* Experience in community college or access focused higher education environments, with a demonstrated commitment to student success and mission aligned philanthropy
* Experience partnering closely with a president, chancellor, or cabinet level leadership on institutional strategy and external engagement
* Demonstrated ability to use advancement data, reporting, and performance metrics to inform strategy and improve fundraising effectiveness

For more than 65 years, Big Brothers Big Sisters of Miami has been creating life\-changing mentoring relationships that empower young people to reach their full potential. This position manages an annual institutional fundraising portfolio of approximately **$3–4 million** and works closely with executive leadership, program staff, and the Board of Directors to secure transformational investments that expand the organization's impact throughout the community.

new_jobs.title: Director of Development
new_jobs.description:
**About First Place AZ**

We are a spirited charitable nonprofit organization advancing a new marketplace of inclusive housing, healthcare, employment and lifelong\-learning options for adults with autism, neurodivergences and/or intellectual/developmental disabilities (I/DD). First Place\-Phoenix, our flagship property, serves as a proof point informing our national work, offering a real\-world site for innovation, learning and demonstration.

First Place AZ is an equal opportunity employer.

**The Opportunity**

Philanthropy at First Place is relational, strategic and mission driven. We don’t just raise money—we build lasting relationships with individuals, foundations and corporations who share our vision and want to be part of something that’s working. Our donors are allies in a movement to reimagine what’s possible for adults with disabilities.

This role is for someone who wants to shape a development function at a pivotal moment. You’ll work closely with the CEO and Board to set philanthropic strategy, diversify and grow revenue, and strengthen the systems that support it all. You’ll be a member of the leadership team, not a siloed fundraiser.

We’re looking for ambition, maturity and aspiration. Someone who sees what First Place is building and is committed to taking it further.

**Who You Are**

You are a seasoned development professional who has outgrown transactional fundraising. You know how to secure major gifts, write winning proposals and steward donors—and you are hungry to do that work in service of a mission you believe in and an organization on the rise.

You think strategically about revenue, not just tactically about asks. You value the entire development ecosystem: individual giving, foundations, corporate partnerships, planned giving, events—and you know how to build a diversified, sustainable funding base.

You are a relationship builder and are as comfortable in a one\-on\-one donor or team meeting as you are presenting to a board. You bring people along.

You lead with clarity and accountability. You have supervised and mentored staff, managed budgets and built systems that work. You communicate performance honestly and use data to guide decisions.

You are ready to be part of a leadership team, assume a seat at the table where strategy is shaped and contribute beyond your functional area.

**What You’ll Do**

**Strategic Leadership**

You will develop and execute First Place’s philanthropic strategy in partnership with the CEO, Board of Directors and leadership team. This includes setting annual fundraising goals and long\-term revenue targets, identifying opportunities to diversify funding streams, preparing development budgets and projections, and monitoring trends to keep the organization ahead of the curve. You will establish policies, procedures and practices that strengthen department efficiency and position the organization for continued growth.

**Revenue Development**

You will lead fundraising across all channels—individual giving, foundations, corporations and planned giving.

For individual donors, you will build and manage a comprehensive giving program, develop annual campaigns and stewardship strategies, identify and cultivate major gift prospects, expand recurring giving , and support the CEO’s donor portfolio.

For foundations and grants, you will oversee the full cycle from prospect research through submission and reporting, develop compelling proposals and budgets, manage funder relationships, and maintain compliance calendars and pipelines.

For corporate collaborators, you will cultivate long\-term partnerships, secure sponsorships for the annual events and initiatives, and ensure fulfillment of sponsorship benefits.

For planned giving, you will lead the growth of the First Place Forever legacy program, recruit, support the Planned Giving Advisory Committee and develop donor engagement opportunities.

**Engagement \& Events**

You will provide strategic leadership for fundraising and cultivation events, including the annual Dimensions® symposium, community tours, donor events, educational webinars and special initiatives. You will recruit and engage volunteers, support Board committees and advisory groups, and coordinate volunteer communications. In partnership with communications and marketing staff, you will shape fundraising campaigns, impact reports, donor communications, annual appeals, sponsorship materials, case statements and website content.

**Operations \& Team Leadership**

You will oversee development operations, including Salesforce database integrity, dashboards and reporting, fundraising KPIs, gift acknowledgment processes and coordination with the finance department. You will supervise development staff, interns and volunteers, providing coaching, mentoring and professional development. You will foster a collaborative, mission\-driven team culture.

**Supervisory Responsibilities**

This role supervises both full\-time and part\-time Development team members, interns and volunteers.

**What You’ll Bring**

* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience required.
* Certified Fund Raising Executive (CFRE) or interest in obtaining certification *preferred*.
* Eight or more years of progressive nonprofit fundraising experience.
* Experience with fundraising for disability, education or human services organizations *preferred*.
* Demonstrated success securing major gifts, grants and corporate sponsorships.
* Experience managing fundraising databases, preferably Salesforce.
* Experience with nonprofit budgeting and financial reporting *preferred*.
* Exceptional written, verbal and presentation skills.
* Strong project management and organizational abilities.
* Experience developing fundraising strategy and annual work plans.
* Ability to analyze fundraising data and communicate performance metrics.
* Experience supervising staff or leading cross\-functional teams.
* Experience planning conferences or large fundraising

**Company Description**  

The Foundation for Food \& Agriculture Research (FFAR) is a nonprofit organization that builds public\-private partnerships to fund audacious research addressing the biggest challenges in food and agriculture. FFAR is committed to providing every person access to affordable, nutritious food grown on thriving farms. The Foundation pioneers scientific and technological innovation by funding research that fills critical research gaps. Ultimately, FFAR’s partnership and results benefit farmers, consumers and the environment.**Position Summary**


The Foundation for Food \& Agriculture Research (FFAR) seeks an entrepreneurial, relationship\-driven Director of Philanthropy to lead the organization's philanthropic fundraising efforts by implementing the newly developed Philanthropy Strategy \& Implementation Plan to advance FFAR's mission and long\-term financial sustainability.  

Reporting to the Vice President of Partnerships \& Development, the Director of Philanthropy will lead fundraising efforts focused on major gifts, family offices, private foundations, corporate philanthropy and other mission\-aligned philanthropic investors. The director will be responsible for implementing a developed robust portfolio of philanthropic prospects, securing significant philanthropic commitments and expanding FFAR's base of unrestricted and flexible funding.  

As FFAR continues to diversify its revenue streams, the director will play a foundational role in building fundraising systems, cultivating strategic donor relationships, supporting executive and board engagement, and positioning FFAR as a premier philanthropic investment opportunity within food and agriculture innovation.  

This position offers a unique opportunity to help shape the future of food and agriculture by securing philanthropic investment in scientific innovation, workforce development and food system transformation. **Duties and Responsibilities**

* Identify opportunities to diversify philanthropic funding sources and increase unrestricted support.
* Monitor fundraising performance and recommend adjustments to strategies, priorities and resource allocation.

* Secure philanthropic commitments ranging from major gifts to transformational investments.

* Conduct donor meetings, presentations and engagement activities that strengthen relationships and advance fundraising objectives.


Foundation \& Institutional Giving (20%)

* Develop and manage relationships with private foundations and institutional funders aligned with FFAR's mission and strategic priorities.

Executive \& Board Engagement (10%)

* Develop briefing materials, relationship maps, meeting preparation materials and solicitation strategies.
* Support board fundraising engagement and ambassador activities.
* Facilitate introductions and relationship\-building opportunities that expand FFAR's philanthropic network.


Donor Stewardship \& Philanthropic Communications (10%)

* Develop meaningful donor engagement opportunities that deepen relationships and encourage long\-term support.
* Partner with Communications and Scientific Program Directors to translate complex scientific work into compelling stories to drive philanthropic opportunities.
* Support development of donor\-facing materials, impact stories, case statements and fundraising communications.


Fundraising Operations \& Portfolio Management (5%)

* Maintain accurate donor, prospect and engagement information within FFAR's CRM system.
* Monitor portfolio health, prospect conversion, fundraising metrics and pipeline performance.
* Contribute to the development of fundraising procedures, reporting systems, dashboards and performance measures.
* Provide regular updates on fundraising progress, pipeline health metrics and opportunities.
**Qualifications**


Education

* Bachelor's degree required.
* Master's degree preferred.
* Degree in business, communications, nonprofit management, public policy, agriculture, food science or systems, sustainability, life sciences or a related field preferred.


Experience

* Minimum of 10 years of progressively responsible fundraising, philanthropy, advancement, development or donor relations experience in agriculture, food, sustainability, etc. is required.
* Demonstrated success securing and stewarding major philanthropic commitments, including six\- and seven\-figure gifts.
* Experience managing major donor port

**POSITION ANNOUNCEMENT**

**New Orleans Recreation Development Commission**

**POSITION: Chief Executive Officer, New Orleans Recreation Development**

**COMMISSION FUNCTION:** The mission of the New Orleans Recreation Development Commission (NORDC) is to advance the physical, mental, and social well\-being of New Orleanians by providing safe and welcoming environments for recreational, athletic, and cultural experiences. The Commission plans, supervises, conducts, and coordinates programs for cultural and physical recreation that will be administered by the New Orleans Recreation Development Commission. NORDC provides a wide range of sports, health, aquatics, day care, and fitness programs for residents and visitors of all ages. NORDC manages senior services, therapeutic recreation, youth camps, teen development, and athletic programs in over 160 facilities city\-wide. The Commission promotes cooperative planning with public and private entities concerned with recreation.

Director of Advancement
new_jobs.description:
Description:

The Director of Advancement provides strategic leadership for all fundraising and advancement efforts at Christian Academy of Knoxville. This position reports to the COO and is responsible for growing philanthropic support through annual giving, major gifts, endowment development, capital campaigns, donor stewardship, and advancement planning while ensuring all fundraising efforts support the mission and strategic priorities of CAK.


Requirements:
**Advancement Strategy**

* Develop and implement a comprehensive advancement strategy that aligns philanthropic efforts with CAK's mission and strategic priorities.
* Establish annual fundraising goals and clearly articulate a compelling case for philanthropic support of CAK.
* Lead the strategy and execution of the CAK Annual Fund, endowment growth, and future capital campaigns.
* Develop and expand planned giving, matching gift, corporate giving, and foundation grant opportunities.
* Identify and implement innovative fundraising strategies and advancement practices that grow and diversify philanthropic support.
* Establish effective donor cultivation, stewardship, and moves\-management processes.

**Major Gifts**

* Identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward major donors and high\-capacity prospects through a strategic portfolio management process.
* Build meaningful donor relationships through regular personal engagement, visits, and intentional cultivation.
* Partner with the Head of School, Board members, and school leadership to identify and pursue major gift opportunities.

**Support Organizations**

* Provide strategic advancement leadership to:
* Warrior Athletic Club (WAC)
* Warrior Parent Association (WPA)
* Arts Council
* Coordinate fundraising priorities and initiatives across support organizations to ensure alignment with CAK's institutional goals and overall advancement strategy.

**Leadership \& Reporting**

* Establish advancement metrics, dashboards, and reporting processes to measure progress and inform strategy.
* Provide regular advancement updates to school leadership and the Board of Trustees.
* Manage the advancement budget and supervise advancement staff.

**Collaboration**

* Partner with Communications and Marketing to integrate advancement priorities and donor messaging into campaigns, publications, digital communications, and stewardship efforts.
* Collaborate with Database Management and Finance to ensure accurate gift processing, donor acknowledgement, prospect tracking, reporting, and financial reconciliation.
* Work collaboratively across school leadership, Admissions, Athletics, Arts, and other departments to align advancement efforts with institutional priorities and foster a culture of philanthropy throughout the CAK community.

*Other duties as assigned*

**Qualifications:**

* Demonstrated commitment to CAK's mission, statement of faith, and Christ\-centered educational environment.
* Bachelor's degree required.
* Five or more years of progressively responsible experience in fundraising, advancement, nonprofit leadership, or a related field.
* Demonstrated success in major gift fundraising, donor cultivation, and advancement initiatives.
* Strong leadership, communication, and relationship\-building skills with the ability to engage a wide range of constituents.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities, exercise discretion, and work independently in a fast\-paced environment.
* Familiarity with advancement technology, donor databases, and fundraising best practices.
* Experience with writing grants or engaging with other foundations for support
* Ability to work evenings and weekends as needed.

new_jobs.title: Director of Development, College of Business and College of Engineering
new_jobs.description:
*Be a trusted, strategic, fundraising partner to enable the University of Nebraska to change lives and save lives.*

* Do you have the drive and desire to partner with professionals and leaders who are a force for good in Nebraska and beyond?
* Have you been a successful fundraiser, well\-versed in the best practices of the profession?
* Do you have the vision and executive presence to help alumni and philanthropic partners fulfill their legacies?
* Do you have a curiosity and appreciation for the University of Nebraska and a strong desire to support the next generation of leaders?


If so, we invite you to apply for this exciting role!


At the University of Nebraska Foundation, we grow relationships and resources that enable the University of Nebraska to change lives and save lives. We believe that our strong commitment to inclusion and respect is essential to accomplishing this mission through our values of teamwork, integrity, and passion for mission. We invite, explore, and celebrate differences to increase productivity, spark innovation and most importantly, accomplish our mission.



You will join the foundation at an exciting time, with the opportunity to make a transformational impact. The foundation is in the public phase of a historic campaign to raise $3 billion to build a better future for Nebraska. The campaign is being led by many enthusiastic volunteers representing all areas of the University of Nebraska System. UNL anchors the campaign, with a goal of $1\.5 billion from enthusiastic donors. ***These positions will support the College of Business or College of Engineering and will be based in our Lincoln office.***


**Ways you will make an impact:**

* Play a critical role in building a robust community of philanthropic support by partnering with university staff and college administrators, leadership, or faculty.
* Plan and implement major gift fundraising strategies, including identification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of major gift donors.
* Foster relationships with alumni, community leaders, and engaged philanthropists to align their philanthropy with the charitable priorities of the colleges.
* Assist in the creation and implementation of the designated campus’ overall development strategy.


**Who we want:**

* **Relationally talented individuals**who make authentic connections, facilitate meaningful conversations, and provide customized solutions to help generous donors fulfill their philanthropic goals.
* **Energetic learners** who are humble, inquisitive, and open\-minded.
* **Self\-motivated and driven**individuals who want to make a difference and are results driven.
* **Collaborative and positive** professionals who can facilitate meaningful conversations with a wide variety of people and are passionate about what they do.
* **Compassionate communicators** who are energized by the opportunity to build long lasting partnerships with others.


**What you need:**

* **Minimum Education:** Bachelor’s degree required. **Preferred Education**: Master’s degree.
* **Experience**: 1\-3 years’ experience in development, fundraising, sales, or a related field with transferrable skills.
* Demonstrated success in the solicitation of annual and major gifts, or transferable skills.
* Ability to coordinate and manage a variety of projects and programs simultaneously.
* Discretion in handling and navigating confidential matters.
* Demonstrated strong oral and written communication skills.
* Candidates will also be evaluated on our core values of passion for mission, integrity, and teamwork.


**What we offer:**

* ***This position will work in our Lincoln office. We offer flexibility for hybrid work******. This position will require travel.***
* Mission\-driven work that changes lives and saves lives.
* A strengths\-based, engagement\-focused, and performance\-oriented culture.
* Ongoing learning and development opportunities for your career growth.
* An award\-winning wellness program.
* 12 weeks of paid medical leave, including maternity and paternity leave.
* 12 paid holidays and generous vacation time.
* A generous retirement match and multiple incentive compensation programs.
* University of Nebraska tuition reimbursement for you and your dependents.


**Who we are:**


* Recognition by the Chronicle of Philanthropy as one of America’s Favorite Charities.
* Founded in 1936, the University of Nebraska Foundation provides support for the campuses in the university system — the University of Nebraska–Lincoln, University of Nebraska at Kearney, University of Nebraska at Omaha, Nebraska College of Technical Agriculture in Curtis, and the University of Nebraska Medical Center and its primary clinical partner, Nebraska Medicine.
* Among public universities, the University of Nebraska ranks in the top 25 nationally for both funds raised and endowed assets under management.
* The foundation has a reputation for efficiency and effectiveness, raising more than $7 for every $1 spent.
* Winner of Gallup’s Don Clifton Strengths\-Based Culture Award.
* Winner of the Governor’s Harvester Wellness Award, the highest honor for corporate wellness.


*The University of Nebraska Foundation is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to a work environment free from discrimination and harassment where all employees feel respected and valued. We will consider all qualified applicants in accordance with applicable law.*


Equal Opportunity Employer  

This employer is required to notify all applicants of their rights pursuant to federal employment laws. For further information, please review the Know Your Rights (https://www.eeoc.gov/poster) notice from the Department of Labor.

new_jobs.title: Director of Development
new_jobs.description:
**Be part of a values\-driven Life Plan Community that has set standards of excellence in retirement living since 1967!**

Foulkeways at Gwynedd was the first Continuing Care Retirement Community (CCRC) in Pennsylvania and the first Quaker CCRC in the United States when we opened our doors in 1967\. With over 50\-years serving the aging population, we continue to reflect the Quaker ideals of friendship, dignity and respect for all our residents and diverse staff.

We are currently searching for an enthusiastic, knowledgeable and detail\-oriented **Director of Development** to join our leadership team.

***Note: This position will be a Part\-Time position. The exact number of hours per week is flexible.***

As the Director of Development for the community, this position has the responsibility to plan, develop, organize, implement, evaluate and direct all philanthropic efforts and programs within the context of Foulkeways mission and philosophies. The Director of Development will develop Foulkeways’ philanthropic vision and will promote a culture of philanthropy among Foulkeways residents, their families, our board and staff.

The Director of Development will cultivate relationships with all stakeholders, especially residents, to encourage and celebrate gift giving to promote Foulkeways’ short\-term and long\-term goals. In addition, the Director of Development will appeal to outside foundations, vendors and the greater community to keep Foulkeways at the forefront of new ideas, technology and projects.

**Responsibilities** of the Director of Development are as follows (these are not inclusive of all job responsibilities):

* Develop philanthropy program, as well as communications plan and supporting materials
* Cultivate positive relationships with company stakeholders
* Develop and maintain lists of potential donors
* Manage the receipt and administration of deferred gifts, such as bequests, gift annuities and trusts
* Report to the Board and Fund Development Committee
* Present Development\-related information to stakeholders through committee participation, group presentations, internals publications, etc.
* Complete administrative tasks as needed

The Director of Development of Foulkeways will promote the mission and values of Foulkeways and will assist in the creation of a culture that celebrates diversity, equity, inclusion and belonging.

**Qualifications Required:**

* Bachelor’s degree in a related field required
* Possess thorough knowledge of charitable giving tax law
* Minimum 5 years prior development experience in a health care setting

**Qualifications Preferred:**

* Certified Fund\-Raising Executive (CFRE) certification preferred
* Excellent computer skills with fundraising software experience preferred

***Do you think you are the perfect fit for this position? If so, please apply today!***

To learn more about Foulkeways, go to https://www.foulkeways.org/

The health and safety of our team members and residents is our top priority. Therefore, we do require that all employees of Foulkeways be vaccinated against Influenza. Exemptions based on religious beliefs and qualifying medical reasons will be considered.

Foulkeways operates on the principle of equal opportunity for all qualified persons regardless of race, color, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, national origin, disability, or any other characteristic protected by law in evaluating any person for employment, training, compensation, promotion, or termination. We operate in accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act. **EOE/ADA**

Work Location: In person

new_jobs.title: Program Director III - Heartland Housing Sanctuary
new_jobs.description:
At Heartland Family Service, we are committed to building a culture that is trauma informed and values and celebrates diversity and inclusion. We believe this allows for better service delivery and innovation, as it encourages our employees to bring various experiences and uniqueness to the workplace.



Trauma Informed Care is an approach to engaging people with histories of trauma that recognizes the presence of trauma symptoms and acknowledges the role that trauma has played in their lives. Trauma Informed Care also acknowledges the effects of working with trauma survivors on our workforce and seeks to build collaborative and supportive working environments and relationships.



If you believe in our mission of creating healthy families and communities, and like a fast pace, collaborative, and team\-oriented environment, then Heartland Family Service is the agency for you.


**SUMMARY DESCRIPTION OF WORK**  

  

This position will work as part of the Sanctuary House team working in close collaboration with the domestic violence sexual assault program service team, all under the umbrella of survivor services. The program provides services designed to meet needs related to immediate safety, trauma recovery, and on\-going support for survivors of human trafficking. This position is responsible for direct oversight of the crisis stabilization shelter, advocacy, and community based programming to include housing services.
